title: Real Numbers
subject: math
audioScript: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Real Numbers. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Euclid's division lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, and Irrational numbers. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

segments.teleprompter: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Real Numbers. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Euclid's division lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, and Irrational numbers. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

Let's lock in the must-know words for Real Numbers. Don't memorise long paragraphs — learn what each term means in one simple sentence. If you can explain these words to a friend, you're already halfway there.
In this part — core concepts — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Real Numbers. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.
Formulas can feel scary, but they're just shortcuts. For Real Numbers, I'll say what each symbol means in plain language, then show when to use it — not just how to copy it from the board.
In this part — solved examples — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Real Numbers. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.
In this part — common mistakes — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Real Numbers. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.

Look at the diagram on screen. Trace it with your finger while I talk. For Real Numbers, the picture is worth more than a page of notes — notice labels, direction arrows, and what changes from left to right.

Time to practise — not to panic. I've lined up about 11 questions on Real Numbers, covering Euclid's division lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, and Irrational numbers. Cover your notes, attempt each question in exam order, then check against the sections above.

Let's connect Real Numbers to real life — not just the textbook. I'll walk through everyday examples for Euclid's division lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, and Irrational numbers: kitchen, lab, transport, or things you see in your neighbourhood.

Before you press play on any video about Real Numbers, grab a notebook. Pause every two minutes and write one sentence in your own words. The links here are curated for Class 10 CBSE — NCERT-style explainers and one-shot revision.

This is your recap if you watched a video on Real Numbers — or if you prefer reading first. I'll walk through the lesson flow: Euclid's division lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, and Irrational numbers, one common mistake, and what you should be able to explain without notes afterward.
Imagine I'm sitting beside you before the board exam. Here's Real Numbers the way I'd explain it to a student who said 'I read it but didn't get it.' Slow pace, simple words, one idea at a time.
Quick recap of Real Numbers. Three things to remember tonight: the big idea, one formula or rule, and one mistake to avoid. That's enough for a confident revision.

sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body:
| 
                   The CBSE envisions a robust, vibrant and holistic school education that will engender excellence in every sphere of human endeavour. The Board is committed to provide quality education to promote
                    intellectual, social and cultural vivacity among its learners. It works towards evolving a learning process and environment, which empowers the future citizens to become global leaders in the
                    emerging knowledge society. The Board advocates Continuous and Comprehensive Evaluation with an emphasis on holistic development of learners. The Board commits itself to providing a stress-free
                    learning environment that will develop competent, confident and enterprising citizens who will promote harmony and peace.केन्द्रीय माध्यमिक शिक्षा बोर्ड विद्यालयों में ऐसी सुदृढ़, जीवंत व सम्पूर्ण शिक्षा की कल्पना करता है जो मानवीय प्रयास के प्रत्येक क्षेत्र में उत्कृष्टता उत्पन्न करवाए। बोर्ड छात्र /छात्राओं को ऐसी शिक्षा उपलब्ध करवाने हेतु प्रतिबद्ध है जो उनमें बौद्धिक, सामाजिक व सांस्कृतिक सजीवता का प्रसार कर सके। बोर्ड ऐसी अधिगम प्रक्रिया व वातावरण उत्पन्न करवाने की दिशा में प्रयासरत है जो भावी नागरिकों को उभरते हुए ज्ञानाधारि…

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Real Numbers**._

**Additional practice set**

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Euclid's division lemma** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Real Numbers.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Euclid's division lemma** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Real Numbers.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Irrational numbers** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Real Numbers.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Irrational numbers** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Decimal expansions** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Real Numbers.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Decimal expansions** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **NUMERICAL (3 marks)** — Using **HCF(a,b) x LCM(a,b) = a x b**, solve a standard board-style problem from Real Numbers. Show all working steps.
- **NUMERICAL (3 marks)** — Using **sqrt(p) is irrational for prime p**, solve a standard board-style problem from Real Numbers. Show all working steps.
- **LONG (5 marks)** — Long answer: Describe the main ideas of **Real Numbers**. Cover at least three syllabus topics and one real-life application.

sections.title: 11. Video Transcript Summary
sections.body:
_Teacher recap — what a strong Class 10 video lesson on **Real Numbers** should cover:_

**Lesson flow (as if narrated on screen):**

1. **Hook** — Start with a daily-life example tied to Real Numbers, not a textbook definition.
2. **Euclid's division lemma** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
3. **Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
4. **Irrational numbers** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
5. **Decimal expansions** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
6. **Formulas** — Write HCF(a,b) x LCM(a,b) = a x b on the board, explain each symbol, then solve one board-style numerical.
7. **Common mistake** — Warn about the error students make most often in exams.
8. **Recap** — Three bullet points to remember before moving to practice questions.

**After watching, you should be able to:**

- Explain Euclid's division lemma in everyday language.
- Draw or describe one diagram related to Real Numbers.
- Attempt at least two short-answer questions without notes.

**Videos linked for this chapter:**

- 🔍 [CBSE class 10 Real Numbers NCERT explained Hindi English](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=CBSE+class+10+Real+Numbers+NCERT+explained+Hindi+English)
- 🔍 [Real Numbers class 10 one shot revision](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Real+Numbers+class+10+one+shot+revision)
- 🔍 [NCERT Mathematics class 10 Real Numbers animation](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=NCERT+Mathematics+class+10+Real+Numbers+animation)

teacherNarration.teleprompter: This is your recap if you watched a video on Real Numbers — or if you prefer reading first. I'll walk through the lesson flow: Euclid's division lemma, Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, and Irrational numbers, one common mistake, and what you should be able to explain without notes afterward.
